# Marketplace Template Fixtures

A reproducible test matrix of **Personas** (souls) and **Agents** (skills) that
exercises the publish → install → sandbox flow across varied dimensions: language,
IM channel, tool complexity, persona constraints, and known name edge cases.

These are **synthetic test fixtures** — no PII, no real credentials. Mock tools only.

## Layout

```
personas/<id>/
  meta.json         {name, description}
  frontmatter.json  soul frontmatter (voice, constraints, compatible_skills)
  body.md           system prompt / identity
agents/<id>/
  meta.json         {name, description}
  <files...>        every other file → a key in the skill `files` map (relative path)
import.sh           packager: dir → SkillExportPayload/SoulExportPayload JSON
dist/               generated by `import.sh pack` (gitignored)
```

## Usage

### Test via UI (Import JSON button)

```bash
./import.sh pack
```

Writes `dist/personas/*.json` and `dist/agents/*.json`. Upload any of them via
**Admin → Templates → Import JSON**. Each file is one `SkillExportPayload` /
`SoulExportPayload`.

### Test via API (bulk import)

```bash
export AGENTSERVER_URL="https://your-deploy"          # default http://localhost:8080
export AGENTSERVER_COOKIE="agentserver-token=<admin session cookie>"
./import.sh import
```

POSTs every fixture to `/api/admin/marketplace/{souls,skills}/import` (admin-only).
Get the cookie by logging into the UI as an admin and copying the `agentserver-token`
cookie value.

## The Matrix — what each fixture tests

### Personas (souls)

| ID | Name | Lang | Formality | Constraints | compatible_skills | Dimension |
|----|------|------|-----------|-------------|-------------------|-----------|
| P1 | Atendente Formal | pt-BR | high | refuse + handoff | A5 | full constraints pt-BR + composition |
| P2 | Casual Helper | en | low | max_turns | A1 | low formality, minimal constraint |
| P3 | Asistente de Ventas | es | medium | handoff | A2 | Spanish, handoff-only |
| P4 | サポート担当 | ja | high | refuse | A6 | **CJK name** (#126) + CJK composition |
| P5 | Minimal Persona | en | — | — (empty `{}`) | — | empty optional fields |
| P6 | Negociação & Cobrança | pt-BR | medium | refuse | A3 | **accent + `&`** (#126) |

### Agents (skills)

| ID | Name | Files | Channels | Dimension |
|----|------|-------|----------|-----------|
| A1 | echo-bot | SKILL.md | dashboard | no-tool pure prompt |
| A2 | weather-lookup | SKILL.md (1 bash tool) | telegram | single tool |
| A3 | doc-search | SKILL.md + prompt.md + references/faq.json | whatsapp | multi-file + references |
| A4 | matrix-notifier | SKILL.md + openclaw.plugin.json | matrix | plugin manifest + matrix |
| A5 | Função Especial | SKILL.md (1 tool) | whatsapp, telegram | **accent name** (#126) |
| A6 | 天気エージェント | SKILL.md | dashboard | **CJK name** (#126) |

### Edge cases covered

- **#126 sanitizeLabelValue** (`internal/sandbox/composition.go`): accents (P6, A5) and
  pure-CJK names (P4, A6). A CJK-only name sanitizes to an **empty** K8s label — the
  real risk is a silent collision between two CJK templates, not a FailedMount.
  Compose P4→A6 (both CJK) in a sandbox to exercise it. See
  `composition_test.go` for the unit-level assertion.
- **Empty optional fields** (P5): soul with `frontmatter = {}`.
- **No-tool skill** (A1): pure prompt, no bash tools.
- **Multi-file skill** (A3): references dir survives the export `files` map round-trip.
- **4 IM channels**: dashboard (A1, A6), telegram (A2, A5), whatsapp (A3, A5), matrix (A4).

## Composition pairs (soul + skill in one sandbox)

`compatible_skills` wires same-language pairs so the composition path is exercised,
not just independent import: **P1→A5** (accent), **P4→A6** (CJK), P2→A1, P3→A2, P6→A3.
